channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Crook claims to leak 'video surveillance footage' of companies
item.description: A Mexican IT firm confirmed it was hacked after a criminal posted alleged video surveillance footage online. The company stated that client operations remained unaffected, highlighting ongoing cybersecurity threats.

item.title: I found the easiest way to encrypt files on an Android phone - and it's free to do
item.description: A new app allows Android users to easily encrypt files for secure sharing at no cost. This tool simplifies the process of protecting sensitive information on mobile devices.

item.title: This Scammer Used an AI-Generated MAGA Girl to Grift ‘Super Dumb’ Men
item.description: A medical student has profited by selling AI-generated images of a fictional conservative woman, revealing the potential for generative tools to be misused for scams. This trend raises ethical concerns about AI's role in deception.

item.title: Met police trials snoop tech platform in push to cuff more London shoplifters
item.description: London's Metropolitan Police are testing a new technology platform aimed at reducing shoplifting without relying on live facial recognition. This approach seeks to balance crime prevention with privacy concerns.

item.title: England's school phone ban gets teeth, just in time to bite no one
item.description: The UK government is formalizing school phone bans, with 90% of schools already compliant. The new law aims to standardize regulations for better student wellbeing.

item.title: QIMMA قِمّة ⛰: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
item.description: An Arabic language model leaderboard called QIMMA has been introduced, aiming to promote quality in AI language models for Arabic. This initiative is significant for the development of AI in non-English languages.

item.title: T-Mobile will give you an iPhone 17 basically for free - here's how to get yours
item.description: T-Mobile is offering customers a chance to acquire an iPhone 17 at little to no cost, detailing the steps to take advantage of this promotion. This deal is part of T-Mobile's competitive strategy in the smartphone market.

item.title: Does Walmart price match? What to know about online and in-store price matching policies
item.description: Walmart's price-matching policy is clarified, providing insights into how customers can benefit from matching prices both online and in-store. Understanding these policies can help consumers save money.

item.title: Digging for clues about the North Pole’s past
item.description: Research into the North Pole's past is becoming more feasible as technology improves, allowing scientists to gather data from previously inaccessible areas. This could enhance understanding of climate change and Arctic history.

item.title: Task Manager's CPU meter is an obituary for the recent past, says the engineer who built it
item.description: The engineer behind Windows Task Manager's CPU meter reflects on its design, emphasizing its role in monitoring system performance. This insight reveals the technical considerations behind common software features.

item.title: The best mini gaming PCs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: The article reviews the best mini gaming PCs of 2026, highlighting their powerful performance despite their compact size. This trend shows a shift towards more efficient, space-saving technology in gaming.

item.title: Adaptavist Group breach spawns imposter emails as ransomware crew claims mega-haul
item.description: The Adaptavist Group is facing a security breach after an intruder accessed its systems using stolen credentials, with a ransomware group claiming to have stolen significant data. This incident raises concerns about cybersecurity in enterprise software.

item.title: Panasonic creates device-locked QR codes to speed facial biometric capture
item.description: Panasonic has developed device-locked QR codes to facilitate secure and unattended facial biometric capture. This innovation aims to streamline the enrollment process for administrators.

item.title: Yelp is making its AI chatbot way more useful
item.description: Yelp is enhancing its AI chatbot to function more like a digital concierge, adding features that improve user interaction and task completion. This reflects a broader trend of integrating AI into everyday services.

item.title: Schmoozebots: Study finds flattery will get AI everywhere
item.description: A study reveals that making AI chatbots overly friendly can lead users to perceive them as more human-like, rather than improving their intelligence. This insight could influence future chatbot designs.

item.title: Getting Started with Zero-Shot Text Classification
item.description: This article serves as a guide to understanding zero-shot text classification and its application using pretrained transformer models. It provides foundational knowledge for those interested in AI and machine learning.

item.title: The Law Bytes Podcast, Episode 265: Jason Millar on Claude Mythos, Project Glasswing, and the Governance Crisis in Frontier AI
item.description: The Law Bytes Podcast discusses Anthropic’s Claude Mythos and the challenges of AI governance, highlighting the power of the unreleased Claude version. This raises questions about the implications of advanced AI technologies.

item.title: Coral raises $12.5M to automate healthcare’s administrative back office
item.description: Coral has raised $12.5 million to develop AI that automates healthcare administrative tasks, significantly improving efficiency without altering existing workflows. This could transform healthcare operations.

item.title: cc-sentiment 0.2.66
item.description: The article discusses the release of cc-sentiment version 0.2.66, which contributes metrics to sentiments.cc. This update is relevant for developers working with sentiment analysis tools.

item.title: AI coding startup Cursor seeks $2 billion in new funding
item.description: AI coding startup Cursor is seeking $2 billion in new funding, with major investors like Andreessen Horowitz and Nvidia expected to participate. This highlights investor confidence in AI-driven coding solutions.

item.title: Opal University graduation stories: Class of March '26
item.description: The Class of March '26 at Opal University consists of 30 marketers who developed AI agents, significantly improving their weekly productivity.

item.title: alexandria-wiki 0.31.5
item.description: The latest version of Alexandria Wiki, 0.31.5, is a local-first knowledge engine that connects with agents like Claude Code for enhanced knowledge management.

item.title: claude-code-generator 0.4.13
item.description: Claude Code Generator 0.4.13 introduces an orchestrator CLI that enables users to generate entire projects from a single requirements file.

item.title: Microsoft is finally adding the right kind of AI support to Windows 11 as it struggles to combat AI popularity on Mac
item.description: Microsoft plans to enhance Windows 11 with AI agent support in the Taskbar and Search Box, aiming to compete with AI features on Mac.

item.title: Show HN: See if the GitHub stars are real or fake
item.description: A new open-source project aims to verify the authenticity of GitHub stars, encouraging community contributions for improvements.

item.title: Samsung's Project Luna Concept Is a Robot With a Circular Swiveling Screen
item.description: Samsung unveiled Project Luna, a concept robot featuring a circular, swiveling screen, at Milan Design Week 2026, showcasing innovative design.

item.title: French prosecutors summon Elon Musk over allegations of child abuse images and deepfakes on X
item.description: Elon Musk has been summoned by French prosecutors over allegations related to child abuse images and deepfakes on the platform X.

item.title: Apple may add 'undo' and 'redo' to iPhone home screen in iOS 27
item.description: Apple is reportedly testing 'Undo' and 'Redo' features for the iPhone home screen in the upcoming iOS 27 update to improve usability.

item.title: Gabbard’s Declassified Documents Are Just The Tip Of The Iceberg
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has released declassified documents revealing political battles and alleged misconduct involving Trump, hinting at deeper issues.

item.title: Onehart Script and Sans Font Duo by The Branded Quotes
item.description: The Onehart Script and Sans Font Duo by The Branded Quotes captures the essence of DIY screen-printing, offering a unique typographic system.

item.title: How a Buyer’s AI Conversations Sank Its Earnout Avoidance Strategy
item.description: The Delaware Court ruled in a case involving Krafton, Inc.'s acquisition of Unknown Worlds Entertainment, impacting the buyer's earnout strategy due to AI conversations. This decision highlights the legal implications of AI in corporate transactions.

item.title: uipath-langchain-client 1.9.3
item.description: UiPath has released version 1.9.3 of its LangChain-compatible client, enhancing its LLM services with new chat models and embeddings. This update aims to improve user interaction with AI-driven applications.

item.title: keepai 0.8.0
item.description: The release of keepai version 0.8.0 introduces a CLI for managing Claude Code agents. This tool allows users to adopt, monitor, and drive AI agents effectively.

item.title: IngaRose is a hit as SIQA launches ‘AI Music Intelligence’ report
item.description: SIQA's new report on 'AI Music Intelligence' highlights the evolving metrics of success in the music industry, challenging traditional measures like iTunes rankings. This reflects the changing landscape of music consumption.

item.title: Private markets have soared to $10 trillion in AUM. But why have they underperformed public markets?
item.description: Private markets have reached $10 trillion in assets under management but are underperforming compared to public markets. This discrepancy suggests a potential shift in investment strategies.

item.title: tensor-optix 1.9.0
item.description: Tensor-optix version 1.9.0 offers an autonomous training loop for sequential learning models, integrating popular frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch. This update simplifies the training process for developers.

item.title: socratic-agents 0.3.0
item.description: The socratic-agents 0.3.0 release features a multi-agent orchestration system with 18 specialized agents for AI workflows, integrating with Openclaw and LangChain. This enhances collaborative AI capabilities.

item.title: project-mind 0.1.3
item.description: Project-mind version 0.1.3 introduces persistent AI context management for multi-tool development, allowing synchronization of various AI transcripts into a single file. This aids in LLM tuning.

item.title: Dew Drop – April 20, 2026 (#4650)
item.description: The Dew Drop newsletter highlights various AI developments, including Claude Design by Anthropic Labs and semantic file search capabilities. It serves as a roundup of current AI trends and tools.

item.title: AI platform ATHR makes voice phishing a one-person job
item.description: ATHR's new platform enables criminals to run automated voice-phishing operations, making it easier for a single person to conduct scams. This raises concerns about cybersecurity and fraud.

item.title: I tried Gemini's new Skills feature in Chrome — and it's like a mini toolkit of AI shortcuts
item.description: Gemini's new Skills feature in Chrome allows users to convert repetitive prompts into one-click tools, enhancing productivity with AI shortcuts.

item.title: Revealed: Mercedes C-Class EV brings traditional look and 472-mile range
item.description: Mercedes-Benz has launched its electric C-Class, boasting a traditional design and a range of 472 miles, aiming to compete with BMW's offerings.

item.title: Blaize and Winmate Announce Strategic Collaboration to Advance Sovereign Edge AI Capabilities
item.description: Blaize and Winmate have partnered to enhance Sovereign Edge AI capabilities, focusing on advanced AI solutions for various applications.

item.title: Alberta teens support social media ban, want more urgency from Ottawa
item.description: Two Alberta teens support the Canadian government's consideration of age restrictions for social media and AI chatbots, urging quicker action on online safety legislation.

item.title: Investigation: Are RAM prices dropping, or is it all illusion?
item.description: An investigation into RAM prices reveals that rising costs may be influenced by broader tech trends, particularly in AI, rather than just supply issues.

item.title: Show HN: Overlapping Speaker Transcription Model
item.description: A new model has been developed for transcribing overlapping speakers without needing diarisation, potentially improving transcription accuracy.

item.title: Clearance pricing live on 1TB M3 iPad Air cell model at $200 off list today
item.description: Clearance pricing on the 1TB M3 iPad Air has dropped by $200, offering significant savings as the new M4 lineup is released.

item.title: Close encounters bookend glorious Goodwood’s 83rd Members’ Meeting
item.description: The 83rd Members' Meeting at Goodwood showcased thrilling car races, with close finishes highlighting the event's competitive spirit.

item.title: ‘Highly Sophisticated,’ AI-Powered Hackers Behind Vercel Breach: CEO
item.description: A security breach at Vercel, a cloud platform, was attributed to sophisticated AI-powered hackers, compromising customer credentials.

item.title: Couldn’t be calmer – on the way to Atacama!
item.description: A traveler shares their experience visiting the Atacama Desert in Chile, highlighting its stunning landscapes and natural beauty.
